title: Kenya ASAL drought — 2020-2023 onset
brief_outcome_free: Kenya ASAL counties (Tana River and the wider arid/semi-arid lands), short-rains season. Pastoralist livelihoods, rain-fed cropping, and rangeland water sources are exposed. The previous season was already below average. You are the DOC drought analyst at the season's start.

rounds.title: Seasonal outlook (T-1 season)
rounds.engine_state: SEAS5 forecast deficit building; current SPI-3 already mild-dry

rounds.title: Deficit escalation + CDI
rounds.engine_state: Risk rising toward Assess; CDI virtual-evidence update sharpens the posterior

evidence_cards.label: SEAS5 forecast deficit probability (vs ERA5 RP threshold)
evidence_cards.teaching_note: Slow-onset signal is STRONG and visible a season ahead — contrast with the convective flood tail case.

evidence_cards.label: Combined Drought Indicator (JRC 14-class)
evidence_cards.source: ICPAC EADW / recompute; 14x5 noisy-likelihood applied to risk posterior
evidence_cards.teaching_note: CDI is NOT a BN parent. It updates the risk posterior multiplicatively = Pearl virtual evidence.

decision.checkpoint_prompt: The forecast deficit and CDI agree a full season ahead. This is a STRONG signal. What DOC level do you set, and what anticipatory action does an early, confident signal justify?

counterfactual.prompt: What if anticipatory action (water trucking pre-position, destocking support) were triggered at T-1 season on the strong forecast signal?
counterfactual.narrative: Slow-onset droughts give long lead. The counterfactual shows the loss-reduction value of acting on a confident seasonal signal rather than waiting for impact confirmation.
